2.5 million?!

@renovate_to_retirement You cannot take out 100k in the tax year at 6 percent - it's just not possible even when shared across 2 directors.  And if you delayed you would pay Corporation Tax which for a limited company with 2 people is effectively income tax with a different name
Kama
@theo___g Ate you some kind of hype guy?  You literally don't see any nuance here at all?

@theo___g to say that people never invest based on the potential of planning is simply not true.  I don't think you are appreciating the challenges associated with developing (or simply redecorating) a grade 2 listed building
@theo___g Keep living in your dream world

5 pound profit on a 37 pound item? How are you making money after shipping to Amazon, VAT and the odd return?

@theo___g You are missing something here.  The cost of 810k was based on the land, the building and the possibility of planning.  If planning fails a number of times you lose the value of that possibility.  So then next option would then be to knock it down - but in this case it's not an option because it's grade 2 listed.  So then the next option is to do it up - in this case you have 7 years of weather damage etc + the much higher cost of development given the regulations and controls surrounding grade 2 listed buildings.  What am I missing?
In a war or revolution where is your money safe?  And what is the opportunity / cost of that option given it's very unlikely?!
@jordanmulliganbrother the measuring device is a Garmin watch - he is just displaying it in Strava.

@fba.charlotte You are effectively paying VAT on the profit though - as VAT on COG and fees is going to be less than what VAT on selling price is.  Plus the cost of Accountant.
